2025-02-25.abstract: Understanding the Alternative for Germany’s success in Sunday’s elections.

2025-02-25.title: Syria’s New Leader Calls for Unity at ‘National Dialogue’
2025-02-25.abstract: Although the conference was called to chart the country’s future, the Kurdish-led militia that controls much of Syria’s northeast was not invited.

2025-02-25.title: Facing Early-Onset Alzheimer’s, She Fought for the Right to Plan Her Death
2025-02-25.abstract: Sandra Demontigny, 45, pushed Quebec to become one of the few places in the world to allow people to choose a medically assisted death sometimes years in advance.

2025-02-25.title: Taiwan Watches Trump Undercut Ukraine, Hoping It Won’t Be Next
2025-02-25.abstract: As President Trump turns toward Russia, Taiwan grapples with its reliance on U.S. support in the face of conflict with China.

2025-02-26.title: Israel Strikes Syria Hours After Country’s Leader Demands Withdrawal
2025-02-26.abstract: The attacks in southern Syria are part of a new policy aimed at protecting what Israel calls its “security zone” in the region. Syria’s new government has condemned that policy.

2025-02-26.title: Chile Declares Curfew as Power Outage Sweeps Country
2025-02-26.abstract: Most residents had power restored by midnight but the situation remained unstable. President Gabriel Boric blamed power companies for not acting faster.

2025-02-26.title: Talks on Syria’s Future Fall Short of Promises, Participants Say
2025-02-26.abstract: The country’s new leaders had billed a two-day “national dialogue” in Damascus as the start of a process to build an inclusive government. But some who attended came away disappointed.

2025-02-26.title: Rejecting Trump’s Call to Annex Their Nation, Canadians Rally Around the Flag
2025-02-26.abstract: While Canadians are not generally known for flag waving, President Trump’s threats have led to a surge in flag sales and buffed up the national symbol’s image.

2025-02-26.title: South Korea Has a Small Baby Bump After Years of Decline
2025-02-26.abstract: South Korea reported a rare increase in births last year as the country grapples with a dwindling population. Its fertility rate remains the world’s lowest.

2025-02-27.title: Possible Remains of Indigenous Women Slain in Canada Found in Landfill
2025-02-27.abstract: The search in Manitoba uncovered possible human remains from two victims of a serial killer, a devastating case that spotlighted an epidemic of violence against Indigenous women in Canada.

2025-02-27.title: Electric Shocks and Tied Crucifixion-Style: The Life of a Scam-Mill Worker
2025-02-27.abstract: Tens of thousands of people from dozens of nations have been trafficked to cyberfraud hubs in Myanmar, where they must con victims or endure brutal punishment.

2025-02-27.title: On a Lawless Tropical Border, the Global Scam Industry Thrives
2025-02-27.abstract: A China-led crackdown on online fraud rescued thousands from Myanmar this month. But this massive business of grift keeps growing.

2025-02-27.title: Indifference or Hostility? Trump’s View of European Allies Raises Alarm
2025-02-27.abstract: Debate is building over just how deep the Trump administration’s antagonism runs, and whether the real goal is to destroy the European Union.

2025-02-27.title: Ignoring Pleas, Thailand Deports Dozens of Uyghurs to China
2025-02-27.abstract: Rights groups criticized the Thai government for sending the Uyghurs, a persecuted Muslim minority, back to China, where they face the risk of torture and imprisonment.

2025-02-28.title: China’s Military Puts Pacific on Notice as U.S. Priorities Shift
2025-02-28.abstract: China is flexing its military muscle in the region to show that it will not wait for the Trump administration to decide how hard it wants to counter Beijing.

2025-02-28.title: South Korean Police Investigate Japanese Woman for Kissing BTS Star
2025-02-28.abstract: ​The woman was accused of kissing him without his consent during an event the K-pop idol held in Seoul last year.

2025-02-28.title: No Trains, No Planes and Huge Protests: Strike Brings Greece to a Halt
2025-02-28.abstract: Two years after the country’s deadliest rail disaster, many are still demanding greater safety and accountability.

2025-02-28.title: How Canadians Are Making Their Anger Toward the U.S. Loud and Clear
2025-02-28.abstract: Canadians are shunning American products and abandoning trips to the United States to protest the economic punishment President Trump has threatened to impose with tariffs.

2025-02-28.title: Milei, $Melania and Memecoins: Unraveling Argentina’s Crypto Fiasco
2025-02-28.abstract: A new cryptocurrency called $Libra bilked investors out of $250 million. It had been promoted by President Javier Milei.
